class.name: GodotVer
class.brief_description: Represents a parsed Godot engine version string.
class.description:
[GodotVer] is a structured representation of a Godot engine version. It extends [SemVer] (and therefore exposes [code]major[/code], [code]minor[/code], [code]patch[/code], [code]prerelease[/code], and [code]build_metadata[/code]) and adds Godot-specific parsing and factory methods. Use it to compare versions, extract individual components, or check whether a build is a known-official release. Note that [GodotVer]'s parser only recognizes [code]dev[/code], [code]alpha[/code], [code]beta[/code], and [code]rc[/code] (optionally followed by digits, e.g. [code]beta1[/code], [code]rc2[/code]) as [code]prerelease[/code] labels. Any other trailing identifier - including [code]stable[/code], [code]official[/code], flavor tags like [code]mono[/code], and commit hashes - is parsed into [code]build_metadata[/code]. [codeblock] var ver = GodotVer.parse_godotver("4.3.stable.official.77dcf97d8") print(ver.major) # 4 print(ver.minor) # 3 print(ver.patch) # 0 print(ver.prerelease) # "" - "stable" is not a recognized prerelease label print(ver.build_metadata) # "stable.official.77dcf97d8" print(ver.is_not_custom_build()) # false - build_metadata contains "official"
var rc = GodotVer.parse_godotver("4.3.0-rc2") print(rc.prerelease) # "rc2"
var custom = GodotVer.parse_godotver("4.3.stable.mycustom.123abc") print(custom.build_metadata) # "stable.mycustom.123abc" print(custom.is_not_custom_build()) # true - build_metadata is non-empty and does not contain "official" [/codeblock]

method.name: create_godotver
method.description: Factory that constructs a [GodotVer] from individual components. [param prerelease] corresponds to the pre-release label and should be one of [code]"dev"[/code], [code]"alpha"[/code], [code]"beta"[/code], or [code]"rc"[/code], optionally suffixed with digits (e.g. [code]"beta1"[/code], [code]"rc2"[/code]); stable releases leave it empty. [param build_metadata] holds extra identifiers that Godot appends to its version strings - typically a flavor and commit hash such as [code]"stable.official.77dcf97d8"[/code], [code]"stable.mono.official.abc123"[/code], or a custom-build marker. Note that [code]"stable"[/code] belongs in [param build_metadata], not [param prerelease].

method.name: is_not_custom_build
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] when this version appears to be a non-official (custom) build, as indicated by a non-empty [code]build_metadata[/code] that does not contain [code]"official"[/code]. Returns [code]false[/code] when [code]build_metadata[/code] is empty or when it does contain [code]"official"[/code]. Note that the method can only speak with confidence about official builds: an empty [code]build_metadata[/code] leaves the classification ambiguous and the method returns [code]false[/code] in that case.

method.name: parse_godotver
method.description: Parses a Godot version string (e.g. [code]"4.3.0"[/code], [code]"3.5.2.stable"[/code], [code]"4.4.dev"[/code], [code]"4.3.stable.official.77dcf97d8"[/code]) and returns a [GodotVer]. The parser recognizes only [code]dev[/code], [code]alpha[/code], [code]beta[/code], and [code]rc[/code] (with optional trailing digits) as [code]prerelease[/code] labels; every other trailing identifier - [code]stable[/code], [code]official[/code], [code]mono[/code], commit hashes, etc. - is captured in [code]build_metadata[/code]. Returns [code]null[/code] if the string cannot be parsed.
